purpose: Vite-served HTML entrypoint for the React renderer. Exists at the project root (per `root: '.'` in vite.config.js) and bootstraps the SPA with a single `<div id="root">` plus a `<script type="module" src="/src/ui/main.jsx">`. Imports two Google Font families (DM Sans, Space Grotesk) used by the design system. After `vite build`, this file is rewritten into `dist/index.html` and loaded by Electron's main process.
gotchas: Loads Google Fonts from a CDN — the bundled Electron app will fail to render fonts correctly when offline; consider self-hosting if offline use is a requirement.
Hard-coded `/src/ui/main.jsx` script URL — Vite rewrites this to a hashed asset path during `vite build`, but the dev server serves it as-is.
Has only `<div id="root">` — no SSR, no fallback content, no noscript message.
No CSP meta tag — content-security-policy is set elsewhere (likely electron/main.js); editing this file alone won't tighten security.
No `<meta name="theme-color">` or PWA tags — Synapse is desktop-only, so this is intentional but easy to miss when copying for a web build.
patterns: Standard Vite + React entrypoint: empty body, single mount node, single ES module script.
Font preconnect pattern (preconnect + crossorigin) for performance — present even though the app is desktop and cold-start performance is dominated by Electron startup.
conventions: Title is the bare product name 'Synapse'.
